stories.headline: OpenAI's head of safety is leaving the company
stories.blurb: Johannes Heidecke is stepping down as OpenAI's head of safety systems just as the company merges its research and safety teams. The move continues a pattern of safety-focused leaders departing OpenAI over the past year, including co-founder Ilya Sutskever and researchers who left to start rival labs focused on AI safety.
stories.why_it_matters: Another senior safety voice exits as OpenAI races to ship products faster
stories.original_headline: OpenAI’s Head of Safety Is Leaving the Company

stories.headline: Meta kills Instagram feature that let anyone make AI deepfakes of you
stories.blurb: Meta pulled its new AI image tool after backlash erupted over letting users generate AI images of any public Instagram account just by tagging them. The feature went live this week with no opt-out for account owners, meaning anyone could feed your photos into Meta's image generator without permission.
stories.why_it_matters: Even Meta decided this crossed a line—consent still matters for training data.
stories.original_headline: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts

stories.headline: Waze now lets you report hazards by talking to Gemini
stories.blurb: Google integrated Gemini into Waze so you can report road issues conversationally instead of tapping through menus while driving. Just say what you see—"there's a pothole in the left lane"—and Gemini will file it. The update also brings natural-language navigation like "avoid highways" without hunting for settings.
stories.why_it_matters: Voice UI that understands context makes safety features actually usable while driving.
stories.original_headline: Waze is getting a bunch of new AI-powered features

stories.headline: DoorDash, Siemens, and Airbnb are switching to Chinese AI models
stories.blurb: Major companies are adopting models from DeepSeek and Alibaba to cut AI costs and reduce dependence on OpenAI and Anthropic. DoorDash uses DeepSeek for customer service routing, while Siemens and Airbnb are testing Chinese models for various internal tasks. The shift comes as US model prices remain high despite recent cuts.
stories.why_it_matters: Chinese AI models are now cheap and good enough to poach enterprise customers from OpenAI.
stories.original_headline: Companies turn to Chinese AI models to cut costs

stories.headline: Intel puts $5.7 billion into its Ireland plant to chase AI chip leaders
stories.blurb: Intel is spending €5 billion to expand its Irish manufacturing facility as it tries to catch up in AI chip production. The move signals Intel's push to reclaim manufacturing leadership after falling behind rivals like TSMC and Nvidia in the race to produce cutting-edge AI processors.
stories.why_it_matters: Intel is betting billions it can still compete in AI hardware after years of losing ground.
stories.original_headline: Intel Invests €5 Billion in Irish Hub to Keep Up in AI Chip Race

stories.headline: Meta commits $50 billion to a single Louisiana data center for AI
stories.blurb: Meta announced its Hyperion data center in Louisiana will cost over $50 billion and consume 5 gigawatts of power—enough to run a small city. The facility, enabled by state tax incentives, represents one of the largest single infrastructure investments in AI computing.
stories.why_it_matters: Big Tech is betting billions on physical infrastructure to keep pace with AI demand.
stories.original_headline: Meta's Louisiana data center investment to reach $50 billion, aided by generous tax incentives

try_this_prompt.title: The research rabbit hole escape
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm researching a topic and I've collected way too much information. I'll describe what I'm trying to figure out below. Give me: the one question that actually matters, three sources that would answer it, and permission to ignore everything else I've bookmarked.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: When you have 47 tabs open and forgot what you were looking for.
